actionable_feedback: Provide the two missing artifacts: burden_mask_definition.tsv and final_decision_table (exact file names expected by the rubric). Ensure these files are included in files_created and are readable previews.
Untruncate and include the full analysis_plan.md so reviewers can verify remediation tables and all rows of the remediation_feasibility table.

Fix the sensitivity-claim inconsistency: either (A) change your textual claim to match the executed perturbation (+/-5%) or (B) rerun and include explicit +/-15% perturbation outputs. The rubric expects explicit +/-15% checks when that claim is made. If you choose +/-15%, produce and surface per-record band-change evidence and set explicit expert-review flags where bands would flip.

Produce a per-record remediation demonstration: apply each remediation option (O1, O2, O3) and relevant combinations to each record and show the resulting per-record normalized_scores and any band changes, with direct row citations back to the packet values. This must be shown in the final_decision_table.

Add an explicit minimality / tie-breaking rationale for recommending O1: show cost-versus-resulting_risk comparisons for all single-option and multi-option sets, and demonstrate that no cheaper option or combination meets the threshold. If multiple options meet the threshold at the same or similar cost, explain operational risk and why O1 is preferred.
Document expert-review assignment rules: for any record that is near band borders or flips under sensitivity perturbations, mark an expert_review flag and explain the decision rule used to set it.

Make the grade derivation transparent: include a short appendix that maps rubric deductions (missing artifacts, sensitivity inconsistencies, partial reproducibility) to the semantic/ numeric grade so grader reasoning is reproducible.
artifact_specific_comments: analysis_plan.md: good reproducible formulas and row-level math, but the preview is truncated (remediation table rows not fully visible).
ActualOutputs.metadata_json.outputs references a burden_mask_definition.tsv path, but that file content was not provided for inspection.
final_decision_table: expected but missing - must include full per-record before/after scores and band assignments for recommended option set.
failed_or_weak_rubric_items: {'rubric_item_id': 'ri_1', 'issue': 'missing_required_artifacts_and_incomplete_or_inconsistent_sensitivity_analysis', 'notes': 'analysis_plan.md present and reproducible in part, but burden_mask_definition.tsv and final_decision_table missing; sensitivity claims inconsistent with metadata.shift_pct'}
failed_rubric_items: {'rubric_item_id': 'ri_1', 'issue': 'missing_required_artifacts_and_incomplete_or_inconsistent_sensitivity_analysis', 'notes': 'analysis_plan.md present and reproducible in part, but burden_mask_definition.tsv and final_decision_table missing; sensitivity claims inconsistent with metadata.shift_pct'}
feedback_summary: The baseline submission contains a reproducible analysis_plan.md with clear formulas and row-level calculations but is missing two required deliverables (burden_mask_definition.tsv and final_decision_table). The sensitivity claims are inconsistent with the recorded sensitivity.shift_pct (artifact text claims +/-15% in places while metadata shows shift_pct=5). The remediation recommendation (O1 at cost 18,600 USD) is within budget and plausibly minimal, but the submission lacks the explicit, row-level demonstration and tie-breaking logic required to prove minimality and to show how each remediation option affects every record. The analysis_plan.md preview was truncated, limiting full verification.

extra_model_fields.grading_notes_summary: Deterministic artifact-contract precheck passed (artifact_contract_score=1.0) because analysis_plan.md existed. Model grader penalized for missing artifacts and incomplete/inconsistent sensitivity outputs, resulting semantic_score ~0.7 and final model-judged score 0.25. Verifier flagged evidence-grounding issues due to truncated preview and sensitivity claim mismatch (+/-15% vs shift_pct=5).
recommended_next_steps_quick: Attach burden_mask_definition.tsv and final_decision_table to files_created and in deliverable_refs.
Rerun sensitivity at +/-15% if you claim +/-15%, otherwise correct textual claims to +/-5% and show results for +/-5%.
Include row-level remediation applications and expert-review flags in final_decision_table.
